metrics.narrative: No quantified value, ROI, or cost-savings figure has been captured anywhere in the record: the CRM's Business_Objectives__c field holds only a placeholder ('Done') and Value_to_Customer__c is blank. The CRM's Identify Pain narrative (dated 23 Mar 2026) describes directional pressures — post-insolvency margin recovery, tail-spend leakage, IPO/JSE audit-readiness — but none of it is tied to a number the buyer has voiced. Until a business case is quantified, the $150,000 ask has no anchor for a finance-led evaluation.

champion.narrative: The EY South Africa team is the primary champion in practice, fronting the entire evaluation on Cell C's behalf ('EY will front', 2 Mar 2026 note) and driving the process through RFP, orals, and follow-up Q&A. Internally, Zeyn Khan (Senior Manager, Strategic Sourcing) and Zuko Ndima (Contracts Specialist) are flagged in the CRM as developing internal champions who reacted positively to the solution and its compliance angle, but neither has a logged direct engagement since that CRM note was last updated on 23 Mar 2026 (~16 weeks ago). Relying entirely on an external evaluation partner for championship is a real exposure if the EY South Africa team's internal read is wrong or Cell C's own committee overrides it.

competition.narrative: Coupa was in the field and withdrew on 30 Mar 2026 ('Coupa pulled out of this one - we have a fair chance', rep note in Next Step History). SpotDraft is named in the CRM's Competition field as of 23 Mar 2026 as the remaining rival, which fits given RFP 2026-005 is scoped as a Contract Lifecycle Management solution, but there has been no fresh mention of SpotDraft in any note or task since, so its current standing in the evaluation is unconfirmed rather than ruled out.

sources: CRM MEDDPICC Identify Pain note, 23 Mar 2026
identify_pain.narrative: Cell C currently runs on SAP with no dedicated sourcing/contracting tool, per a direct Salesforce field, consistent with the manual/fragmented-process pain theme in the CRM's Identify Pain note (post-insolvency margin pressure, tail-spend leakage, JSE/IPO audit-readiness risk, supplier dependency, dated 23 Mar 2026). None of this has been directly quoted by a buyer in a call we've read, since the one real evaluation touchpoint (8 May 2026 orals) was not recorded, so the depth and buyer-owned language behind the pain remain unconfirmed.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifact (MSA, Order Form, SOW, or term sheet) has surfaced yet. This is expected: the deal is still in Formal Evaluation, a stage short of vendor selection where paper process normally begins. There is no evidence yet to assess.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Shaheen De Vries, Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement)
economic_buyer.narrative: Shaheen De Vries, Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement, is the senior-most named contact on the account and the closest match to an economic buyer. She personally emailed the Zycus/EY group on 24 Feb 2026 to reschedule a demo session, copying the Zycus account owner's own manager on the thread. She is understood to have been present for the 8 May 2026 orals and demo, but there has been no confirmed direct engagement with her since 24 Feb 2026 (~19 weeks ago), and her posture has only been relayed through EY since. Salesforce's own Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c booleans are unticked, so this reads as identified-but-thin rather than confirmed sponsorship.

sources: Next Step / Next Step History (dated entries, 27 Jan-1 Jul 2026)
decision_process.narrative: This is a multi-vendor evaluation run through the EY South Africa team as the fronting/evaluation partner: a short intro on 27 Jan 2026, a demo on 16 Feb 2026 that Cell C's team liked, RFP 2026-005 issued with a 16 Mar 2026 deadline, Coupa's withdrawal on 30 Mar 2026, orals and a full demo before the panel on 8 May 2026, a written Q&A round closing 29 May 2026, and EY's own estimate of naming a preferred vendor within about a month of the orals (target ~8 Jun 2026). That estimate has now lapsed by about a month with no announcement.

decision_criteria.narrative: Cell C's evaluation team, through sourcing coordinator Mpho Mabaso, sent Zycus a written follow-up on 27 May 2026 spelling out three concrete criteria: a confirmed implementation timeframe, a fully itemized pricing breakdown by cost component (licensing, implementation, support, training, maintenance), and a defined user access and governance model (org-wide viewing rights, template-population rights, and a roughly 5-15 person super-user/approver layer with legal and executive/chief-level sign-off per business unit). Zycus answered all three within two days (29 May 2026). This is specific, buyer-articulated criteria, not a generic RFP checklist.

day_summary.overall: The only thing that happened today was a quiet pushback of the close date by a month, from end of July to end of August — no calls, emails, or notes accompanying it. Ball is effectively in our court to find out why and re-engage.

flags: Multi-thread inside Cell C beyond EY: Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct engagement from internal Cell C stakeholders (Zeyn Khan, Zuko Ndima, Shaheen De Vries all confirmed in roles but engagement strength unclear). Risk: if EY's influence is weaker than assumed or an internal Cell C exec objects, the deal stalls. High-leverage move: secure a direct 1:1 with Shaheen De Vries (the economic buyer) to confirm her sponsorship and diagnose any internal hesitation, independently of EY's fronting role.

Reframe the business case as an IPO-readiness enabler: Cell C's JSE listing mandate requires audit-ready procurement (a regulatory forcing function), but no quantified ROI or savings case has been articulated. Re-engage with a compliance-led narrative (S2P = de-risk the IPO by delivering audit-grade spend visibility and controls) and quantify the value (e.g. tail-spend reduction %, contract-cycle compression, compliance-audit cost avoidance) to justify the investment in a post-insolvency budget environment.

Offer a phased or cost-optimized commercial structure: Given Cell C's post-insolvency turnaround context and the 40-day silence (possible budget sensitivity), propose a Phase 1 focused on compliance/audit readiness (iContract + iSource) with deferred Phase 2 for eProcurement/eInvoicing, or explore a term-extended payment structure to ease cash-flow impact. This lowers the entry barrier and aligns with their capital-light MVNO model while still delivering the IPO-readiness mandate.

EY-mediated engagement has gone silent for 41 days (last contact 27 May, no reply to 1 Jul outreach). Zycus is over-reliant on the external fronting partner with no direct Cell C internal champion actively driving the decision. Secure a direct call with Shaheen De Vries (EB) to validate budget, timeline, and decision authority, bypassing the EY bottleneck.

Decision Maker and approval chain unclear. Shaheen De Vries (EB) may require CFO, board, or exec-committee sign-off for a material procurement investment in the IPO context, but no visibility into the approval process. Map the final signatory and approval gates before the close date tightens further.
Build a quantified business case now (cost/margin impact) to give EY and Shaheen De Vries ammunition for an internal sign-off, since no numbers have been articulated to date.
Convert Zeyn Khan and Zuko Ndima from passive, previously-positive contacts into active internal champions who can independently confirm status while EY is silent.
Coupa's March withdrawal opened the field; confirm SpotDraft's current status and get a CLM-vs-S2P differentiation note to EY before the decision closes.
best_practice_check.summary: Reduce reliance on a single external relay and re-anchor the business case while the decision is stalled.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa was the more recent, verified competitor and it withdrew from the RFP on 30 Mar 2026, which the rep read as leaving 'a fair chance.' SpotDraft is the only other named rival, per the CRM MEDDPICC Competition field updated 23 Mar 2026, but no call evidence since has confirmed whether it remains active in the evaluation; treat it as the current strongest known threat only because it is the sole one still unresolved.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Coupa puled out of this one - we have a fair cahnce
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa is out. Win by re-engaging EY and Shaheen De Vries, reframing the S2P investment as an IPO-readiness enabler (audit-grade procurement de-risks the JSE listing), and offering a phased or cost-optimized commercial structure to address any post-insolvency budget sensitivity.

competitors.name: SpotDraft
competitors.how_we_win: Position the full source-to-pay suite (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing) against SpotDraft's narrower CLM-only footprint.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A committee evaluation that engaged seriously through May 2026 (orals, demo, detailed follow-up questions, and Coupa's exit) has gone quiet for the past six weeks, right as the evaluation partner's own one-month decision estimate lapsed with no word.

deal_risk.text: The EY South Africa team's self-set decision timeline is now roughly a month overdue with no announcement and no explanation — the single biggest open risk on this deal.
Zycus has no confirmed direct read on the named economic buyer's (Shaheen De Vries) position since 24 Feb 2026 and is reliant on the EY South Africa team relaying Cell C's internal sentiment.
No quantified ROI or cost-savings case exists to support a $150,000 investment at a cost-sensitive, post-insolvency, IPO-readiness-focused account.

win_position.text: Cell C's evaluation team requested a detailed cost-component breakdown and implementation-timeline commitment on 27 May 2026 (Mpho Mabaso, via Task) and Zycus answered within two days (29 May 2026, Next Step History) — the kind of late-stage commercial diligence a buyer usually only runs on a vendor still genuinely in contention.
The only other named competitor, Coupa, withdrew from the evaluation on 30 Mar 2026 (~14 weeks ago), per the rep's own note ('Coupa pulled out of this one - we have a fair chance').

The EY South Africa team told Zycus after the 8 May 2026 orals that a preferred-vendor decision was expected within about a month; that window closed around 8 Jun 2026 and, as of 9 Jul 2026, roughly a month has passed with no announcement and no confirmed reason for the delay.

There has been no direct engagement with the named economic buyer, Shaheen De Vries, since a scheduling email on 24 Feb 2026; Zycus has relied entirely on the EY South Africa team to relay Cell C's internal position for over four months.
SpotDraft, a purpose-built CLM competitor, is still on record as competition per a 23 Mar 2026 CRM note that has not been refreshed or corroborated by any call evidence since.

deal_momentum.text: The rep's 1 Jul 2026 outreach to the EY evaluation team asking for a status update has gone unanswered for 8 days as of 9 Jul 2026, extending a buyer silence that now stands at 43 days since the last inbound reply (27 May 2026).

The most recent two-way exchange was substantive: Cell C's sourcing coordinator sent a detailed written follow-up on 27 May 2026 covering implementation timeline, pricing structure, and the access/approval model, and Zycus closed it out within two days.
No forward-dated milestone or future meeting is currently on record; the deal's only remaining pace-setter is the EY South Africa team's own lapsed one-month decision estimate.

customer_commitment.text: Cell C invested a full orals-and-demo session (8 May 2026) plus a detailed written follow-up round (27-29 May 2026) — real evaluation effort, not a passive courtesy demo.
No stated business case, ROI figure, or compelling event has been voiced by the buyer to anchor the $150,000 spend, and no future session is currently scheduled.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: An AI-branded demo landed well early, but no subsequent evaluation content has tested AI capability specifically.

items.quote: Kindly confirm the estimated timeframe required for full implementation of the proposed solution, including onboarding, configuration, and go-live.
Please clearly stipulate each cost component and indicate what each charge relates to (e.g., licensing, implementation, support, training, maintenance, etc.).
items.criterion: Comprehensive, itemized pricing by cost component

items.quote: A controlled access model with approximately 5-15 'super users/approvers' responsible for legal review and executive/chief-level approvals within each business unit.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Cell C's post-orals follow-up named three concrete evaluation criteria; Zycus responded to all three within two days, but no buyer confirmation of satisfaction has been received since.

ML iAnalyze is the sole AI SKU and it's a spend-analytics product (machine learning categorisation) rather than the generative-AI/agentic stack. AI is not the headline of the deal.
• Formal Evaluation stage with EY South Africa engaged as advisor (jakob.schleu@za.ey.com, EY India team in support) and a strong Cell C buying committee including s.devries, lkekae, zndima and rfourie. Three meetings scheduled Feb-Mar 2026 but all three returned 'not_recorded' state in Avoma, so we cannot verify any customer-spoken AI sentiment from transcripts. BD_Engagement is Engaged per SFDC.
• Low confidence: SF Description is null and all Avoma meetings unrecorded. The score rests on product mix (ML iAnalyze hints at analytics AI) plus EY's involvement as an advisory partner. Default DOWN at 5/6 boundary held given the complete lack of explicit AI evidence from transcripts. Would move UP only if a recorded discovery surfaces buyer-side AI demand or RFP issuance specifies AI capability.
